commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=9f37f5d720f4e6be896be5acc2903b41fc9af0de)
commands.review_summary:
Adds optional `diagnostics.laneWaitWarnMs` configuration for command queue lane wait warnings, with schema label, docs, runtime snapshot lookup, and command-queue tests.

PR surface: Source +19, Tests +156, Docs +16. Total +191 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main is source-reproducible because `enqueueCommandInLane` falls back to a hard-coded `2_000` when no per-entry `warnAfterMs` is passed; I did not run live gateway proof in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Contributor or maintainer follow-up is needed because the PR lacks real behavior proof and the new config surface needs contract/reload fixes before merge.

target.title: feat: configurable diagnostics.laneWaitWarnMs threshold
target.body:
## Summary

`diagnostics.laneWaitWarnMs` is now configurable. The "lane wait exceeded" warning's 2-second floor was hard-coded, so contended lanes flooded the diagnostic log even when waits were within normal operating range. The default stays at 2_000 ms; users on chatty lanes can raise it.

## Why this matters

Filed in #14747: the `lane wait exceeded` line at `command-queue.ts:347` fires for every lane wait > 2s, and there's no way to raise that floor short of patching the source. On environments where some lanes routinely cross 2s during legitimate work, this fills the diagnostic log with noise that buries actual stuck-lane signals. Making the threshold configurable lets operators tune it to their environment without losing the diagnostic when something is genuinely stuck.

## Changes

- `src/config/zod-schema.ts`: adds `diagnostics.laneWaitWarnMs: z.number().int().positive().optional()`
- `src/config/schema.labels.ts`: documents the new field
- `src/process/command-queue.types.ts`: extends `CommandQueueRuntimeConfig` with the diagnostics shape
- `src/process/command-queue.ts`: adds `resolveDefaultLaneWaitWarnMs()` that reads `getRuntimeConfigSnapshot().diagnostics?.laneWaitWarnMs` with a 2_000 ms fallback; carries the resolved value as `warnAfterMs` into the diagnostic line so the threshold that fired is visible
- `src/process/command-queue.test.ts`: covers per-entry threshold, default fallback, and dynamic runtime-config update
- `docs/gateway/diagnostics.md`: documents the new field, default, and reset semantics

## Testing

Vitest in `command-queue.test.ts`. Default behavior unchanged for users who don't set the field.

Fixes #14747

AI was used for assistance.
target.author: mvanhorn
